name: Thermocoagulator
description:
The Thermocoagulator is a handheld device used to treat human tissue lesions. The unit is designed based on the World Health Organization’s guidelines and recommendations for fighting the growing epidemic of cervical cancer. It is a type B thermal coagulator which is a compact, portable, battery-powered mobile device which can be used in hospital and non-hospital rural healthcare locations. It is reusable and provided non-sterile and designed to perform low-power destruction of human pre-cancer cervical or other tissue with high temperatures by tissue contact with a heated probe tip.
• Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ): 1 device
• Estimated Lifespan: 4 years
• Seller Name: Liger
• Seller Location: United States
• Warranty Term: 2 years
• Regulatory Approval: CE, US FDA, ISO 13485
key_features: Heated Probe Tip - Contains a heating element, which is heated to 100°C (212°F) on the default setting for the destruction of human tissue. The probe tip should only be applied to tissue, which is intended to be ablated. The TC probe shaft is not intended to deliver heat. The probe shaft and sidewall tip has a maximum temperature of 43°C (109°F). Contact with the pro...
Usability - Automated procedure timing with visual and audible indicators – no watch or clock required. Can be set to 20, 30, 40, or 60 seconds and 80, 90, 100, or 120°C.
Exchangeable Probes - Heat quickly (5-8 seconds) that can be autoclaved for sterilization.
Portable - Lightweight with no tether wires or cables required to connect probe to device and no external power supply required for treatment.

name: LifeWrap Non-Pneumatic Anti-Shock Garment (NASG) – Size Medium/Large
description:
The LifeWrap Non-Pneumatic Anti-Shock Garment (NASG) is a revolutionary first aid device designed to combat shock and manage postpartum and obstetric hemorrhage effectively. Weighing only 1.5kg, this lightweight, washable, and reusable compression garment can be used up to 144 times per unit, making it a sustainable and cost-effective solution for medical emergencies. Crafted from neoprene and secured with Velcro over a foam compression ball, the LifeWrap NASG exerts circumferential counter-pressure once fitted, significantly reducing blood loss and effectively reversing hypovolemic shock by enhancing blood flow to critical organs. This innovative device is essential for healthcare providers and emergency responders, offering a life-saving intervention for hemorrhagic conditions.
• Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ): 1 piece
• Seller Name: LifeWrap International
• Seller Location: Hong Kong
• Warranty Term: 2 years
• Lifespan: Can be used to 144 times
• Regulatory Approval: CE, ISO 13485, ISO 9001
• Product Variations: LifeWrap Non-Pneumatic Anti-Shock Garment (NASG) – Size Small
key_features: Globally Trusted - The LifeWrap NASG has been delivered to dozens of countries with potential to be used on hundreds of thousands of women to manage complications with postpartum hemorrhage. The LifeWrap NASG has been widely delivered by VIA and used by providers throughout Africa, South Asia and Latin America, where maternal death rates remain high.

Usability - With minimal training, frontline health providers are able to successfully apply the garment to the bleeding patient. Once the mother’s bleeding is controlled, she can be safely transported to a hospital for obstetric care.
Reusable Quality - The LifeWrap NASG has been independently tested for up to 144 uses, putting the LifeWrap NASG at the forefront of quality and reusability.

Patient Accessibility - Importantly, the NASG does not need to be removed for uterine examinations or vaginal procedures, with the abdominal part only being opened for abdominal surgery. The patient’s vital signs often immediately improve upon application.

Transportable - LifeWrap NASG is lightweight and easily folded and packed, and once in use can stabilize patients for up to three days while patients are transported to a health center with the ability to administer blood transfusions and provide definitive medical or surgical interventions.

Expert Developed - The manufacturer works closely with the UCSF (University of California, San Francisco) and the Safe Motherhood Program and particularly with Dr. Suellen Miller, the leading global NASG expert and advocate of the LifeWrap NASG.

specifications.Lifetime: • Each LifeWrap NASG can be used up to 144 times
specifications.Product Dimensions:
• Tummy Total Width: 120 cm
• Hip Total Width: 96.5 cm
• Total Length: 123 cm

name: Pumani bubbleCPAP
description:
The Pumani bubbleCPAP is an innovative respiratory support device designed for newborns and children, offering a low-cost yet highly effective solution for continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP) therapy. Engineered for durability, this device is built to operate flawlessly for years, coming equipped with spare parts and a comprehensive repair manual for straightforward, annual maintenance. The Pumani stands out by delivering therapeutic pressure levels comparable to the more sophisticated and expensive gold-standard bCPAP devices found in the United States, all while maintaining simplicity and cost-effectiveness.
The device’s operation is ingeniously simple, consisting of a flow source, a pressure source, and patient tubing. The air flow is generated by an air pump, whereas the pressure necessary for effective CPAP therapy is created using a water bottle. Remarkably, the Pumani’s only moving parts are small diaphragms that are inexpensive to replace, costing less than $1, and are included with the device. Demonstrating its global impact, the Pumani is currently in use in 35 countries worldwide, making it a vital tool in enhancing respiratory care for the most vulnerable patients across the globe.
• Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ): 1 device
• Lifespan: Can be used for 4 years, assumed 262 working days per year
• Seller Name: Hadleigh Health Technologies
• Seller Location: United States
• Warranty Term: 2 years
• Regulatory Approval: Kenyan Pharmacy and Poisons Board, CE, ISO 13485
key_features: Usability - Clear labels on front panel of the Pumani allow for simple use. A new user can be trained to operate the Pumani in less than one day, and a user manual and repair manual are included with the system.

Adjustable - The Pumani is capable of delivering flow from 5 to 10 L/min and pressure from 5-8 cm H2O. Adjusting these settings is as easy as turning a knob or filling a water bottle. The device also has the ability to blend room air with oxygen from a concentrator or oxygen tank.
Controls - The Pumani is easy to operate thanks to a streamlined control panel design.

name: The IRIS by Liger: Colposcope and Coagulator in One
description:
The Iris by Liger is a combined Screen-and-Treat device used to treat and diagnose pre-cancerous and human tissue lesions in the cervix using thermal ablation. It comes with two 19mm flat probes, one 19mm nipple probe, and one 16mm flat probe.
• Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ): 1 kit 
• Seller Name: Liger
• Seller Location: United States
• Warranty Term: 2 years
• Regulatory Approval: US FDA
key_features:
How do I clean the Probe? - The full high-level sterilization is given in the IFU that was sent with each unit on page 5 column 2. Many users follow the method below for cleaning and Hi-level disinfection of the probes:
Place the silicon cap firmly over the connector end. Clean any debris off the probe with mild detergent and water using a soft cloth or soft brush, then rinse

What's the expected lifespan of the batteries? - The batteries are expected to last more than 2 years. Testing data has shown NO degradation of battery life even with heavy use
over a two year period. This included 1300 heating cycles on the two batteries with a recharge on the battery every 30 cycles.

name: MTTS Firefly Phototherapy
description:
The MTTS Firefly Phototherapy is a state-of- the-art phototherapy device designed for effective treatment of neonatal jaundice right in the mother’s room, supporting mother-child bonding and breastfeeding.
• Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ): 1 device
• Seller Name: MTTS
• Seller Location: Vietnam
• Warranty Term: 1 year
• Regulatory Approval: CE, ISO 13485, EN 60601-1:2006 + A1:2013, EN 60601-1-2:2015, EN 60601-2-50: 2009, EN 60601-1-6:2010, ISO 10993-Part 10, ISO 10993-Part 5
key_features: Can I use this with an incubator? - No, the MTTS Firefly is not for use with an incubator.
Can I use the Firefly with a radiant warmer? - No, the MTTS Firefly is not intended for use with a radiant warming device.

name: MTTS Impala Ventilator
description:
The MTTS Impala Ventilator is a compact mechanical ventilator for neonatal, pediatric and adult patients. It is suitable for intensive care units and ambulances.
• Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ): 1 device
• Seller Name: MTTS
• Seller Location: Vietnam
• Warranty Term: 1 year
• Regulatory Approval: ISO 13485, EN 60601-1-2:2015, EN 60601-1:2006/A1:2013
• Product Variations: Impala Ventilator with Stand
key_features: Design - The Impala Ventilator is portable, which makes it suitable for use in emergencies or intensive care wards. Designed with a built-in battery for 4 hours of backup power. Further, the Impala has an easy-to-use touch screen and no time restriction on use due to the gas tank limitation.

Effective - The Impala Ventilator features electronic mixing for easy, independent adjustment of flow rate and FiO2. It also has ACV, SIMV and CPAP modes, and controlled target PIP and consistent PEEP.

Compatible - Impala combines a compact, quiet, and easy-to-operate technology with precise control of gas flow delivery and oxygen concentration. For added patient safety, Impala incorporates a full complement of alarms.
Cost Efficient - Impala combines proven clinical effectiveness in respiratory care and low cost consumables with exceptional value.

name: MTTS Lightmeter
description:
The MTTS Lightmeter provides precise measurement of the intensity of the blue light spectrum that is necessary to treat jaundice in infants.
● Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ): 1 device
● Seller Name: MTTS
● Seller Location: Vietnam
● Warranty Term: 1
● Regulatory Approval: ISO 13485
key_features: Quality Design - By measuring the wavelength spectrum from 400 to 500 nanometers, the Lightmeter gives a reliable irradiance measurement and helps to comply with the American Academy of Pediatrics Guidelines. Spectral response chosen to match wavelength used for Jaundice treatment. Two-sided measurement for convenient measurement of double-sided phototherapy device...

Versatile - The Lightmeter can be used to measure the intensity of any type of phototherapy device used for the treatment of hyperbilirubinemia in newborn babies, including single-sided and double-sided machines. The Lightmeter can also be used as a tool for clinical decision making, allowing the care giver to determine how to position the baby, maximize light...
Displays/Controls - Reflective LCD screen can be read even under bright lights orion sunlight. Touch sensitive button cannot wear out or break.

• Lifetime: Up to 120h operating time

name: MTTS Dolphin CPAP
description:
The MTTS Dolphin provides continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP) therapy to support the airways of preterm and low-birth weight newborns, who can breathe spontaneously but inadequately.
• Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ): 1 device
• Seller Name: MTTS
• Seller Location: Vietnam
• Warranty Term: 1 year
• Regulatory Approval: CE, ISO 13485, EN ISO 13485:2016, EN 60601-1:2006/A1:2013
key_features: Humidifier - The Dolphin humidifier automatically refills for 3-days of continuous operation and has a small heated volume for quick warm up. The heater power is automatically adjusted to maintain correct humidity and minimize rain-out.

Patient Circuit - Composed of robust but flexible silicone tubes. Completely reusable and most parts are autoclavable. If an autoclave is not available, parts can be chemically disinfected. The circuit includes an internal heater wire and temperature sensors.

Pulse Oximeter - Pulse-oximetry is integrated into the device. The Dolphin uses Masimo SET® technology for accurate, reliable, and robust measurements. There is a simple user interface and alarm settings and no multi-level menus or complicated button sequences.

Air Compressor - The integrated air compressor means there is no need for an external air source. It is completely integrated, meaning no messy external wires or tubes and is a diaphragm type for clean, long lasting air supply.

specifications.Performance:
• PEEP/CPAP Type: Bubble
• Pressure Range: 0 to 10 cm H2O
• Gas Mixing Type: Electronic
• Oxygen Percentage / FiO2: 21 to 100%
• Flow Rate: 4 to 9 LPM
• Air Compressor Type: Blower
• Power Supply: Integrated, 12V DC
• Gas Supply Oxygen Input Range: 25 to 60 psi
• Oxygen Connector: DISS 1240
• Humidifier Type: Passover
• Humidity: >90% RH

name: InnAccel Saans CPAP
description:
The SAANS device is an infrastructure-independent, easy-to-use, sensor-based device that provides CPAP, HFNC, and Resuscitation therapies to neonates and pediatric patients.
• Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ): 1 device
• Seller Name: InnAccel
• Seller Location: India
• Warranty Term: 1 years
• Regulatory Approval: ISO 13485
key_features: Adaptability - The SAANS device is infrastructure independent. Its portability and ease-of-use ensures that it can be used efficiently in a hospital setting as well as in a transport setting. It can be used across multiple wards in the hospital; from NICU to labor to the ICU.

Efficiency - Within one device, one can achieve CPAP, HFNC and resuscitation Therapy for neonatal and pediatric patients. It is also Compatible with universal AC input with backup battery providing 4-6 hours of power.
Usability - The SAANS is compatible with multiple Oxygen sources: central line, cylinder and Oxygen concentrators. It also comes with an in-built flow generator and air-oxygen blending.
Displays and Alerts - It comes with alerts that signal changes in pressure, flow, FiO2, leakage and disconnection. It also has a large display and single control knob.

specifications.Output Accuracy:
• <2% Full-Scale Value
• 1x Saans Main Unit
• 1x Trolley
• 1x Servo-controlled Humidifier with clamp
• 1x Bubble Jar with clamp
specifications.Parameter Control and Display:
• Digital control and display of Flow Rate – set and actual values
• Digital control and display of FiO2 – set and actual values
• Digital control and display of PIP and PEEP (Resuscitation mode)
• Digital display of Pressure (CPAP mode)
• Integrated 8-inch LCD Display
• Parameter control with single touch knob
• External pressure control using PEEP valve or Bubble Jar options
specifications.Device Output (Flow and Pressure):
• Flow: 2 to 60 LPM
• Pressure (CPAP mode): 2 to 12 cm H2O
• PIP (Resuscitation mode): 5 to 40 cm H2O
• PEEP (Resuscitation mode): 2 to 10 H2O
• FiO2: 21% – 100%
• Separate modes for CPAP, HFNC, and Resuscitation delivery

name: Liger Electrosurgical Generator
description:
The ESU-110 Electrosurgical Generator is an simple, battery powered and portable device that has been specifically tuned for the LLETZ (or LEEP) procedure for excision of pre-cancerous lesions.
• Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ): 1 kit
• Seller Name: Liger
• Seller Location: United States
• Warranty Term: 2 years
• Regulatory Approval: ISO 13485, US FDA
key_features: Unable to ship to - Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Canada, Croatia, Cyprus, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Ireland, Italy, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, Turkey, United Kingdom
Portable - The total weight of the device with accessories is a combined 8lbs. This has allowed Dr’s to take the device to the most remote locations in the world.
CUT & COAG - Using a standard 3 prong electro surgical pencil you can perform both cut and coag functions.
Smoke Evacuation - The port at the front of the devices allows for a HEPA carbon filter and smoke evacuation tubing to be connected to a Graves Speculum.
Battery Operated - Closed loop DC circuit limits risk of accidental burns to patient from ground contact. No mains power infrastructure required for procedure.
Single button Interface - The device is specifically tuned for LEEP/LLETZ procedure. No power adjustments are necessary.

name: MTTS Beluga Resuscitator
description:
The Beluga Resuscitator uses positive pressure to inflate the lungs of an unconscious patient who is not breathing, in order to keep them oxygenated and alive.
• Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ): 1 device
• Seller Name: MTTS
• Seller Location: Vietnam
• Warranty Term: 1 year
• Regulatory Approval: 60601-1:2006/A1:2013, EN 60601-1-2:2015, ISO 13485
key_features: Usability and Safety - Beluga combines a compact, quiet, and easy-to-operate technology with precise control of gas flow delivery and oxygen concentration. For added patient safety, Beluga incorporates a full complement of alarms.
Design - Beluga uses latest technology and together with user-friendly touch screen interface allows clinicians to save time and focus on the actual problems to solve.
Quality - Beluga resuscitator is a precise and reliable device that provides safe and effective therapy, high reliability components and long-life batteries.
Versatility - Beluga can be used for emergencies where quick action is required as well as in controlled hospital environment for prolonged period of time.

Effectiveness - Beluga resuscitator features electronic mixing for easy, independent adjustment of flow rate and FiO2. Beluga combines proven clinical effectiveness in respiratory care and low cost consumables with exceptional value.

Using the Beluga Resuscitator as a CPAP - Beluga is a t-piece resuscitator with adjustable PIP and PEEP settings. This means that yes, it can be used as CPAP once these values are set in certain way (PEEP as desired with PIP simultaneously set higher than PEEP). The difference between PIP and PEEP will determine the flow rate. PEEP can be set from 0 to 20 cm H2O. However, the Beluga is not a CPAP and customers should be aware of the following limitations: No bubble effect, No heated gas, No humidification. These features would have to be provided externally (for example through an external humidifier).

specifications.Performance:
• Power Characteristics: 100 – 240 VAC, 50-60 Hz
• Manometer Accuracy ± 0.5 cm H2O
• Peak Inspiratory Pressure (PIP): 5 – 50 cm H2O
• Positive End Expiratory Pressure (PEEP): 1 – 20 cm H2O
• Delivered Oxygen Concentration: 21 – 100%
• Integrated Air Compressor: Blower
• Battery: Li-ion, 12V-4000mAh

name: Ellavi Free-Flow Uterine Balloon Tamponade
description:
The Ellavi Uterine Balloon Tamponade (UBT) is a minimally invasive, easy-to-use, and effective intervention for treating and managing severe postpartum bleeding.

• Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ): 25 kit
• Seller Name: Sinapi
• Seller Location: South Africa
• Warranty Term: 0 years
• Regulatory Approval: CE, Ghana FDA, ISO 13485, Kenyan Pharmacy and Poisons Board

SKU: VIA_Ellavi | Category: Hospital & Clinic, Maternal & Obstetric, Maternal, Newborn and Child Health | Tags: Obstetrics Emergency, Pregnancy Complications, PPH
key_features: Design - The Ellavi uterine balloon tamponade comes with a supply bag that has a 1000ml fluid capacity. The balloon is thin walled which ensures low expansion pressure.
Tubing - The Ellavi UBT comes with 2.25m tubing installed, this allows for vertical hydrostatic pressure control and rapid filling of the balloon.
T-valve Tap - The T-valve tap allows for the system to be either fixed or free-flow.
Effective Balloon - The UBT is equipped with a large enough, medical grade elastomer balloon (>750ml) to fill the entire uterus and apply equal pressure to the uterus wall once filled.

specifications.Regulatory Certificates: CE Certified, ISO 13485, Ghana FDA, Kenyan Pharmacy and Poisons Board

faqs.answer: At start of treatment, hang the supply bag between 1m and 1.5m for rapid filling. During monitoring, adjust the height of the supply bag according to the measured blood pressure of the patient (SBP > Drainage only).
faqs.question: How high should the supply bag be?

faqs.answer: An expelled balloon may occur due to insufficient fluid volume, improper placement, or if the uterus contracts too strongly. Ensure proper positioning and adequate fill volume.
faqs.question: What are the reasons for an expelled balloon?

faqs.answer: No, vaginal packing is not required with the Ellavi UBT system. The balloon design provides adequate tamponade effect without additional packing.
faqs.question: Does the vagina require packing with the Ellavi UBT?

faqs.answer: Yes, the patient can be transported and transferred with the Ellavi UBT in place. The free-flow design allows for safe patient movement during treatment.
faqs.question: Can the patient be transported/ambulatory/transferred with the Ellavi UBT in situ?
